  "findings": [
    "Personnel working directly under a suspended load",
    "Failure to establish and maintain a controlled access zone under the lift path"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Order an immediate stop to all lifting operations",
    "Direct all personnel to clear the area beneath the suspended load",
    "Lower the load to a safe, stable position on the ground",
    "Establish physical barricades and warning signs to prevent entry into the fall zone",
    "Conduct a safety stand-down and review the lift plan with the rigging crew and ground workers"
  ],
  "rationale": "Working under a suspended load is a critical safety violation that poses an immediate threat of severe injury or death. OSHA regulations strictly prohibit employees from standing or passing under suspended loads unless specific, highly controlled conditions are met (such as during steel erection, and even then, exposure must be minimized). The operation must be halted immediately to protect worker lives.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.1425",
    "OSHA 1926.753"
  ]
}
